title: Payton Pritchard’s huge night leads short-handed Celtics over streaking Hawks: 7 takeaways
author: Tom Westerholm
bullets: Payton Pritchard led the short-handed Celtics with 36 points in their 109-102 win over the Atlanta Hawks.
Jayson Tatum played 36 minutes, contributing 25 points, 12 rebounds, and 5 assists despite struggling with his shooting.
Celtics player Jaylen Brown missed the game with Achilles tendinitis and is considered day-to-day.

title: Why did Jayson Tatum come back this season? The Celtics’ success had ‘a lot’ to do with it.
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Jayson Tatum returned to play for the Boston Celtics just over ten months after Achilles surgery.
Tatum's decision was influenced by the Celtics' strong position in the championship race this season.
He has averaged 9.2 rebounds per game since returning, though shooting percentages are below career averages.

title: Police: Man killed in Lowell apartment complex shooting
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: A man was shot and killed at an apartment complex on Pawtucket Boulevard in Lowell on Friday afternoon.
The shooting occurred after an altercation between two armed men who knew each other from the building.
The incident is under investigation by the Middlesex DA’s Office, Massachusetts State Police, and Lowell Police Department.

title: Report: Connecticut Sun to relocate to Houston with purchase by Rockets owners
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: The Connecticut Sun will relocate to Houston in 2027 after being purchased by Rockets owners, the Fertitta family.
The Sun's reported sale for $300 million marks a WNBA record, as noted by ESPN's Alexa Philippou.
The team will be renamed the Houston Comets, resurrecting the city's former WNBA team disbanded in 2008.

title: 5 must-watch movies & TV shows streaming right now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: 'Daredevil: Born Again' Season 2 starring Charlie Cox is streaming on Disney+.
'From the World of John Wick: Ballerina' features Ana de Armas and is available on HBO Max.
'Bait', a new series featuring Riz Ahmed, is now streaming on Prime Video.

title: Celtics’ Jaylen Brown (left Achilles tendinitis) out vs. Hawks
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Jaylen Brown is out for the Celtics game against the Hawks due to left Achilles tendinitis.
Head coach Joe Mazzulla says Brown's injury status is day-to-day, with ongoing treatment provided.
Nikola Vucevic is also out, while Derrick White and Neemias Queta are available for the game against Atlanta.

title: ‘Where does it end?’: Readers react to gas-powered leaf blower bans
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Arlington and Lexington towns have phased out gas-powered leaf blowers due to noise and emissions concerns.
A Boston.com poll shows 73% of respondents oppose the ban, citing practicality issues for large properties.
Supporters of the ban cite noise and air pollution; some suggest restricted usage hours as a compromise.

title: What to know about the highly mutated new COVID strain found in Mass. wastewater
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: The BA.3.2 COVID variant, known as the 'cicada' variant, has been detected in Massachusetts wastewater.
BA.3.2, first found in South Africa in 2024, has spread to 23 countries and 132 US wastewater samples.
Current COVID vaccines may be less effective against BA.3.2, but WHO expects them to still prevent severe disease.

title: DCF workers who authorized removal of kids in Waltham could stand trial, judge rules
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: A federal judge ruled that a lawsuit against DCF workers Katheryn Butterfield and Candice Gemski can proceed.
The couple, Josh Sabey and Sarah Perkins, allege unconstitutional seizure of their children without evidence of abuse.
The children were removed in 2022 after a hospital visit, despite a DCF investigation finding no evidence of abuse.

title: 3 New England restaurants among the most anticipated openings, according to Food & Wine
author: Katelyn Umholtz
bullets: Food & Wine magazine lists three New England restaurants among its 12 most anticipated spring 2026 openings.
Willie's by Chef Jamie Bissonnette, known for global-inspired pizzas, is now open in Beacon Hill, Boston.
Ladyfish, a Maine seafood pop-up by Chef Jordan Rubin, opens May 1 in Portland, Maine.
